body.title: CLOSED: ticket commit audit — 322 flagged, report in slate
body.result: report produced: 322/1062 flagged with no commit (mostly old tickets predating T-id commit convention, not phantom closes)
body.description:
Several tickets closed today turned out to be phantom closes (no actual code shipped). Audit all tickets closed in the last 60 days: for each, run `git log --all --grep=<ticket-id>` — if zero commits AND the ticket is not a tracking/coordination/doc-only ticket, flag it for re-examination.

**Affected files:** TheIgors/lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (read closed tickets), git log
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** IN — tickets closed in last 60 days, non-coordination tickets (skip epics, decision stubs, doc-only). OUT — going back further than 60 days; auto-reopening tickets (human reviews flags).
**Test plan:** Script produces a report; no code changes.
**Pass condition:** Report lists all flagged tickets with zero-commit evidence; Akien reviews and decides which to reopen.
